jokes.text: D-ohh! (Screams)
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation and scream
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - perfectly character-integrated but routine at this point in the series

jokes.text: Books? Books are for squares. We're now a multimedia learning center for children of all ages... but mostly bums.
jokes.punchline: The honest admission that it mostly serves bums
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about modern libraries with good economy and unexpected honesty

jokes.text: Visual gag: All the bums sleeping in the library
jokes.setup: Library employee's comment about serving mostly bums
jokes.punchline: Cut to reveal multiple homeless people sleeping
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual callback that proves the previous line true - excellent structure and economy

jokes.text: Visual gag: Books titled 'Everybody Poops The Video' and 'Yu-Gi-Oh! Price Guides'
jokes.setup: Looking for actual books in the modernized library
jokes.punchline: Finding only dumbed-down media and commercial guides
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of cultural dumbing-down with specific, funny examples

jokes.text: I have to score at least a pumpkin sticker or better on it
jokes.punchline: Pumpkin sticker as the minimum acceptable grade
jokes.explanation: Nice detail about elementary school grading system that fits Lisa's character

jokes.text: I'm hen-e-ry viii, I am hen-e-ry viii, I am, I am / I've been eating since 6:00 A.M. / For dessert I'll have dinner again / my name's synonymous with gluttony / I'll always eat a turkey or a ham...
jokes.punchline: Homer-as-Henry singing about his gluttony to a familiar tune
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration - Homer's gluttony perfectly matches Henry VIII, with clever musical structure

jokes.text: I've been eating since 6:00 A.M. For dessert I'll have dinner again my name's synonymous with gluttony
jokes.punchline: Extreme eating schedule and self-awareness of gluttony
jokes.explanation: Good character work combining Homer's appetite with historical Henry VIII

jokes.text: stop singing that song! We all know who you are.
jokes.punchline: Wife's exasperated, practical response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge-like deflation of Homer's grandiosity with meta-awareness and great economy

jokes.text: Her majesty, margarine of aragon!
jokes.punchline: Catherine of Aragon becomes 'Margarine of Aragon'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that connects Marge's name to the historical figure with perfect economy

jokes.text: I just planted my seed in your womanly dirt.
jokes.setup: Queen asking why he's out of bed
jokes.punchline: Crude euphemism for sex delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Homer-appropriate crudeness that fits the historical context while being uncomfortable

jokes.text: Your majesty, I know you want a son, but must we discuss my womb in front of the entire court?
jokes.setup: Henry's crude public discussion of procreation
jokes.punchline: Marge's mortified but reasonable objection
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Marge's embarrassment and practicality shine through historical context

jokes.text: as royal physician, it is my learned opinion that her womb is filled with sea serpents.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous medieval-style diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Nick incompetence applied to medieval medicine - absurd but character-consistent

jokes.text: Could a girl belch like this? (Huge belch)
jokes.setup: Elizabeth trying to prove she's masculine enough to be heir
jokes.punchline: Demonstrating with an enormous belch
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy that plays against Lisa's usual character while serving the historical parody

jokes.text: Too much jerkin' your merkin?
jokes.setup: Henry wondering why he can't have a son
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude pun about medieval pubic wigs and masturbation
jokes.explanation: Clever historical wordplay that's perfectly Bart-like in its crude precocity

jokes.text: Get out of my dreams and into my wife!
jokes.punchline: Parody of Billy Ocean's 'Get Out of My Dreams and Into My Car'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture reference twisted to fit the historical context with perfect economy

jokes.text: I could have married the king of france. He wasn't so preoccupied with procreation. Ting-a-ling-a-ling. Know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Catherine complaining about Henry's obsession with heirs
jokes.punchline: Subtle implication that the French king was impotent or gay
jokes.explanation: Subtle historical joke with good innuendo, though less character-specific than ideal

jokes.text: Modern wench magazine dubbed me 'anne of the child-bearing hips.'
jokes.punchline: Parody of modern magazine celebrity coverage applied to medieval times
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronistic joke mixing modern celebrity culture with medieval marriage politics

jokes.text: My son could cartwheel out!
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous image of a baby cartwheeling during birth
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual image delivered with perfect economy and Homer's enthusiasm

jokes.text: Your majesty, your feelings are valid, but I'm afraid marriage takes a lot of hard work. (Snaps fingers) and who needs that?
jokes.setup: Marriage counselor starting to give serious advice
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning professional ethics with a finger snap
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of therapy tropes with perfect timing and character consistency

jokes.text: I say trade in that lemon and get biz-zay. Whoo-hoo!
jokes.punchline: Using car dealership metaphor and slang for sex
jokes.explanation: Good character work mixing modern slang with historical setting, very Lenny-like

jokes.text: Sire, there's no such thing in the cath-diddly-atholic church.
jokes.punchline: Ned's signature 'diddly' speech pattern applied to 'Catholic'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Ned's speech pattern perfectly preserved in historical context

jokes.text: Where divorce will be so easy, more than half of marriages will end in it.
jokes.setup: Henry deciding to create his own church
jokes.punchline: Accidentally predicting modern divorce statistics
jokes.explanation: Clever anachronistic joke that works on multiple levels - both historically absurd and accidentally prophetic

jokes.text: And I think a celibate italian weirdo knows a lot more about marriage than you.
jokes.setup: Thomas More defending the Pope's authority on marriage
jokes.punchline: The obvious contradiction of celibate clergy advising on marriage
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about Catholic marriage authority with good sarcastic delivery

jokes.text: And because you stuck to your principles, I'm going to canonize you. I can see my house...
jokes.setup: Henry praising Thomas More's principled stand
jokes.punchline: Using 'canonize' to mean execution by cannon, More's distant view comment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark wordplay on 'canonize' - both religious honor and literal cannon execution. Excellent structure and originality.

jokes.text: It's not your fault. It's just that you came out the wrong sex and ruined everything. So grow a penis or get lost.
jokes.setup: Henry explaining divorce to his daughter like a modern parent
jokes.punchline: Blaming the child for being female and impossible demand
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny parody of divorce explanations, mixing modern therapy speak with medieval sexism

jokes.text: Well, why can't your heir be female? Or-or why can't we elect our leaders? I wonder if I could canonize a child.
jokes.punchline: Henry considering executing her for the suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Lisa's progressive thinking and Henry's violent response, callbacks to canonize joke

jokes.text: Hey, I invented divorce. How did you get half of everything? You should have invented the pre-nup.
jokes.setup: Henry shocked at losing half his kingdom in divorce
jokes.punchline: Modern pre-nup joke applied to the inventor of divorce
jokes.explanation: Clever modern legal concept applied anachronistically with good ironic structure

jokes.text: I get Ireland?! (Laughing)
jokes.setup: Catherine learning what she gets in the divorce settlement
jokes.punchline: Her delighted reaction to getting what's historically considered the worst part
jokes.explanation: Good historical joke about Ireland's perceived value, delivered with appropriate ironic enthusiasm

jokes.text: And by the power vested in me by you just now, I pronounce you king and trophy queen. In the name of the henry, the hank and the holy harry. Amen.
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony between Henry and Anne Boleyn
jokes.punchline: Trinity parody using Henry's name variants and 'trophy queen' description
jokes.explanation: Clever religious parody mixing Henry's vanity with modern 'trophy wife' concept

jokes.text: your head lives for five seconds afterwards, so I left a magazine in the basket.
jokes.setup: Henry about to execute Anne Boleyn
jokes.punchline: Considerate gesture based on execution urban legend
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark and absurd - taking the execution legend and making it weirdly considerate. Excellent originality and economy.

jokes.text: Oh, my horoscope! 'Today will bring welcome new changes into your life.' Wrong!
jokes.setup: Anne reading magazine while being executed
jokes.punchline: Horoscope's ironic optimism right before death
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark irony with excellent callback structure - horoscope optimism vs. execution reality

jokes.text: Sir scratchy, I accuse you of conducting a catholic mass! Lord itchy, I accuse you of not signing the act of succession!
jokes.punchline: Using Itchy & Scratchy names for nobles
jokes.explanation: Nice Simpsons universe integration mixing cartoon names with historical persecution

jokes.text: Do you, jane seymour, take this king to be your lawfully wedded husband till your first little spat? Oh, I do! I double do! Henry is the ginchiest!
jokes.punchline: Realistic short-term wedding vows and outdated slang enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good realistic take on Henry's marriage pattern plus period slang for extra anachronistic humor

jokes.text: (Laughing) It's funny 'cause you're king.
jokes.setup: Henry asking 'How would you like to be queen for a day?'
jokes.punchline: Explaining why the joke works
jokes.explanation: Good meta humor about explaining jokes, economical and self-aware

jokes.text: I told you I was a dude. You didn't dump me, I dumped you!
jokes.punchline: Treating death like a breakup with defiant last words
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframing of death as relationship drama with perfect economy and attitude

jokes.text: My track record! I've had ten sons. Now take a ride on the king-maker!
jokes.punchline: Boastful sexual offer with crude metaphor
jokes.explanation: Appropriately crude and uncomfortable for the situation, though less character-specific

jokes.text: I'm 95! You could use a butter knife, for god's sake!
jokes.setup: Henry about to execute the old woman
jokes.punchline: Practical complaint about execution method efficiency
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with practical complaint about execution efficiency - good economy and originality

jokes.text: well, we're running out of pikes to stick your wives' heads onto. I'll show you running out of pikes! Hey, what do you know? You were right.
jokes.setup: Messenger delivering bad news about pike shortage
jokes.punchline: Henry proving him wrong by executing him, then acknowledging the accuracy
jokes.explanation: Good dark logic - Henry disproving the messenger's point while simultaneously proving it

jokes.text: My whole life I was looking for that one woman whose execution could bring me happiness. Now I realize I was just beheading myself for divorcing you.
jokes.punchline: Mixing execution puns with relationship therapy language
jokes.explanation: Clever mixing of execution wordplay with modern relationship confession language

jokes.text: see ya in hell, fat man!
jokes.setup: Catherine appearing to reconcile and comfort dying Henry
jokes.punchline: Violent farewell while smothering him with pillow
jokes.explanation: Great dark twist - apparent reconciliation becomes revenge with perfect economy

jokes.text: England's power was never greater, and british actresses always had a role to play when they got to a certain age.
jokes.setup: Historical narration about Elizabeth I's reign
jokes.punchline: Meta-joke about older British actresses playing historical queens
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on Hollywood casting patterns with good observational accuracy

jokes.text: And I just came in here to trip nerds for nothin'. Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Everyone praising the library as a great resource
jokes.punchline: Nelson realizing he could have been productive instead of just bullying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment with his signature laugh - shows growth while staying in character

jokes.text: In elementary school, we girls learned about Sacagawea while the boys were learning math.
jokes.punchline: Implying girls got less valuable education
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary about educational sexism, economically delivered

jokes.text: and some embedded journalists
jokes.punchline: Modern war reporting term applied to 1804 exploration
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic joke applying modern media terminology to historical context

jokes.text: Long have we awaited the coming of the white man... and carl.
jokes.setup: Solemn pronouncement about white men arriving
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of Carl as separate category
jokes.explanation: Great comedic timing treating Carl as a separate prophetic category from white men

jokes.text: Thanks, and welcome to the united states of america. Have a flag. And while you're at it, cover your nakedness and worship our lord.
jokes.setup: Greeting Native Americans as new Americans
jokes.punchline: Casual cultural imperialism checklist
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of American colonization process presented as casual checklist

jokes.text: In our language, her name means 'little know-it-all who won't shut her maize hole.'
jokes.punchline: Name translation revealing Lisa's know-it-all nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration - perfectly captures Lisa's personality through fake Native American name translation

jokes.text: (French accent): I will come with you-- because by myself, ze darkness, she scares me.
jokes.setup: French fur trader husband introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Admission of cowardice in bad French accent
jokes.explanation: Good Milhouse characterization showing his cowardice through historical character

jokes.text: I don't know why I ever sold you to him.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to selling daughter as regrettable business decision
jokes.explanation: Dark historical humor treating sale of daughter as mundane regret

jokes.text: I'll tell you what's poisonous-- your attitude.
jokes.setup: Sacagawea listing poisonous plants and animals
jokes.punchline: Carl's sassy comeback about her attitude
jokes.explanation: Standard comeback line that works in context but lacks originality

jokes.text: But at least people will always remember the expedition of lewis and clark and tweedleburger.
jokes.punchline: Adding ridiculous name to the famous expedition
jokes.explanation: Absurd name addition to famous expedition - simple but effective

jokes.text: And they said you couldn't open a bar in kansas. Well, nothing can crush the frontier spirit. That'll do it.
jokes.setup: Moe expressing optimism about frontier spirit
jokes.punchline: Immediately crushed by building collapse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe timing and character - optimism immediately destroyed by reality

jokes.text: Well, nothing can crush the frontier spirit. (Building collapses) That'll do it.
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicted by building collapse
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure with perfect timing, good visual comedy

jokes.text: We got the job because we own a compass. Turns out the needle was just painted on.
jokes.setup: Sacagawea asking how they became explorers
jokes.punchline: Revealing their only qualification was fake
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic explaining their incompetence with perfect economy and structure

jokes.text: Can't we at least have one pity scalp?
jokes.setup: Being told not to kill the explorers
jokes.punchline: Asking for minimal consolation violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating scalping as minor consolation prize

jokes.text: For the last time, (slowly): Those are salmon!
jokes.setup: Carl talking about mermaid sex with fish
jokes.punchline: Sacagawea's exasperated correction delivered slowly
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa frustration moment with appropriate escalation and delivery

jokes.text: You know, you could be a little more grateful to us for civilizing you.
jokes.setup: Sacagawea having saved their lives repeatedly
jokes.punchline: Carl's oblivious claim about civilizing her
jokes.explanation: Sharp ironic commentary on colonial attitudes - perfect obliviousness to reality

jokes.text: Oh, warm, wooly rock, you're my only friend.
jokes.punchline: Talking to what she thinks is a rock
jokes.explanation: Sets up bear reveal nicely, good character moment of loneliness

jokes.text: Of course we did. We'll never forget you, Pocahontas. Sacagawea! Gesundheit.
jokes.punchline: Treating correction as sneeze
jokes.explanation: Clever structure with name confusion and gesundheit punchline, well-crafted

jokes.text: I say we give this lovely land a name worthy of its beauty: Eugene, oregon!
jokes.setup: Dramatic naming of beautiful Pacific Northwest
jokes.punchline: Mundane, unimpressive city name
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax joke - grand setup undercut by mundane reality

jokes.text: You can trade it in at the bank for a real dollar!
jokes.setup: Presenting Sacagawea dollar as great honor
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's not actually real currency
jokes.explanation: Sharp commentary on the uselessness of commemorative coins as honors

jokes.text: hello, vienna! Are there any aficionados in the house?
jokes.punchline: Modern rock concert crowd interaction
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic mixing of classical and rock concert conventions

jokes.text: Oh, little salieri, why don't you go play with the other three untalented members of our family-- tito, randy and jermaine?
jokes.setup: Salieri asking to be featured performer
jokes.punchline: Comparing family to Jackson 5 with Michael as the talented one
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture parallel between Mozart family and Jackson 5 dynamics

jokes.text: oh, lord, why did you give such transcendent talent to such an undeserving fool? Because you are ug-ly!
jokes.punchline: Mozart's crude, simple answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response to Lisa's intellectualizing - crude but effective character contrast

jokes.text: I want to see you all next week at my concert in... Krakow! (Laughter)
jokes.punchline: Krakow as undesirable venue
jokes.explanation: Standard geographic joke about undesirable tour location

jokes.text: wow, it's sold out-- mincing room only!
jokes.punchline: Period-appropriate version of 'standing room only'
jokes.explanation: Clever period wordplay updating modern phrase to 18th century mannerisms

jokes.text: beans, oh, beans, delicious in your mouth / but watch out when beans come out down south / tooting, some call it pooting / it's air polluting, the gas comes shooting / right from your butt, butt, butt / butt, butt, butt, butt, butt, butt, buttttt...
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated classical music reveals to be fart song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character integration - turning high art into juvenile humor while maintaining musical structure

jokes.text: Guten tag, everybody! Guten tag, dr. Nick.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick arriving to treat sick Mozart
jokes.punchline: His signature greeting routine in German
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick routine adapted to historical setting - good character consistency

jokes.text: I can tell from here you have too much blood. Let's get you covered in leeches!
jokes.punchline: Medieval medical practice delivered with his usual incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Nick characterization applied to period medicine - his incompetence fits medieval practices

jokes.text: Now in the morning, you'll be good as new... or dead! But the important thing is, we'll know.
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of uncertainty with twisted priorities
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick logic - finding the silver lining in medical failure with perfect character voice

jokes.text: But now that I'm dying young... I'll be cool forever! eat my pantaloons!
jokes.punchline: Modern understanding of dying young for fame plus period insult
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mixing of modern celebrity death culture with period-appropriate insult - very Bart-like

jokes.text: (To tune of fifth symphony): Haw haw haw haw... haw haw haw haw
jokes.punchline: Nelson's signature laugh set to classical music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration - Nelson's laugh perfectly fits Beethoven's Fifth rhythm. Excellent musical comedy.

jokes.text: All I know is, the guy who played mozart was also in animal house. Now there's a movie with good music. Animal house, house, house... nobody ever went to class / then we saw donald sutherland's ass
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude preference for Animal House over Amadeus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - dismissing high culture for lowbrow comedy with his characteristic crude enthusiasm

jokes.text: Animal House, house, house... nobody ever went to class, then we saw Donald Sutherland's ass...
jokes.punchline: Crude summary of movie set to melody
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer lowbrow humor, crude but character-appropriate
